name: Borough boundaries
desc: New York City's five boroughs — Manhattan, the Bronx, Brooklyn, Queens and Staten Island — each also a state county.

name: Community districts / boards
desc: 59 local areas, each with an appointed community board that weighs in on land use, the budget and city services.

name: City Council districts
desc: The 51 districts that each elect a member of the City Council, the city's lawmaking body.

name: Community school districts
desc: 32 districts that group the city's public elementary and middle schools, run by the Dept. of Education.

name: Police precincts
desc: The 77 NYPD patrol areas, each with its own station house and commanding officer.

name: Fire battalions
desc: FDNY operational areas, each overseeing a cluster of nearby fire companies.

name: Fire divisions
desc: FDNY's largest operational areas; each division contains several battalions.

name: Fire companies
desc: The response area of a single FDNY engine, ladder or squad company.

name: Sanitation districts
desc: Areas the Dept. of Sanitation uses for collection, plowing and street cleaning; they mirror the community districts.

name: Health center districts
desc: Areas the Health Dept. uses to report neighborhood health statistics; each groups several health areas.

name: Health areas
desc: The Health Dept.'s smallest units for tallying vital statistics like births and deaths.

name: Business improvement districts
authority: NYC Dept. of Small Business Services
desc: Commercial zones where property owners tax themselves to fund extra sanitation, security and marketing.

name: State Assembly districts
desc: Each elects one member of the New York State Assembly, the lower house in Albany.

name: State Senate districts
desc: Each elects one member of the New York State Senate, the upper house in Albany.

name: Congressional districts
desc: Each elects one member of the U.S. House of Representatives.

name: Election districts
desc: The smallest voting unit; each is assigned a poll site and nests inside a State Assembly district.

name: Neighborhood tabulation areas
desc: Statistical "neighborhoods" the City Planning Dept. built from census tracts to report population data.

name: Community district tabulation areas
desc: Census-tract groupings that approximate the 59 community districts for reporting census data.

name: Census tracts (2020)
desc: Small, stable areas the U.S. Census Bureau uses to report demographic data.

name: ZIP code tabulation areas
desc: The Census Bureau's mapped approximations of USPS ZIP codes.

name: Historic districts
desc: Areas designated by the Landmarks Preservation Commission, where exterior building changes need approval.
